title: Remote Access and Network Management Paths · Packet Forensics
summary: Provide secure operator, vendor, and user connectivity with recoverable in-band and out-of-band paths. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a least-privilege management access plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare site-to-site and client-to-site VPNs, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.
Choose split or full tunnel by traffic path, inspection, performance, privacy, DNS, and local-network risk.
Choose SSH, GUI, API, or console management with encrypted transport, identity, least privilege, and logging.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network access management. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Net Site Client Vpn trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare site-to-site and client-to-site vpns, clientless access, authentication, authorization, and route scope.

content.text: For Net Site Client Vpn, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Net Site Client Vpn precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Artifact under review: a complete first version of a least-privilege management access plan while every earlier competency remains active.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Net Site Client Vpn, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-inband-outofband-1nj8kf" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Inband Outofband into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: net-inband-outofband-1nj8kf
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable probable cause]
# command: choose one safe read-only simulator command
# observation: [replace with the result that supports or rejects the hypothesis]

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Inband Outofband applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare in-band and out-of-band management paths and preserve isolated recovery without creating an unmonitored bypass.
